Impact of 1.5°C quarterly summer temperature increase. 'Evaluating the regression coefﬁcients pertaining to average tem- peratures in our central model (shown in Column 1 of Supple- mentary Table S2) at the baseline temperature observed in our dataset on the three hottest months of the year on average across countries (23.5 C), and given a 1.5 C temperature increase, indi- cates an impact of 0.17%-points...implying an impact of 0.49%-points which is closely consistent.'
